raw_response:
I agree with the conclusion drawn by Agents 1 and 2. The key point here is maintaining alignment with the WHO definitions, specifically their emphasis on a pure-tone average threshold exceeding 90 decibels, typically rounded up to ensure accuracy in practical applications. Since option C specifies >91 dB, it accurately reflects the stringent requirements set forth for defining profound hearing loss according to these international health organization guidelines.
